Title: The maize ZmMIEL1 E3 ligase and ZmMYB83 transcription factor proteins interact and regulate the hypersensitive defence response

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RING E3 ubiquitin ligase with zinc finger CHY, CTCHY, and RING2 domains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses Rp1-D21-mediated hypersensitive response and suppresses Arabidopsis RPM1D505V-induced HR; increases repression of some VLCFA biosynthetic gene expression via regulation of ZmMYB83
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; FoMV-VIGS silencing in maize; MG132 proteasome inhibition assay; in vitro autoubiquitination assay; coimmunoprecipitation; BiFC; immunoblot; RT-qPCR; ion leakage assay
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZmMYB83; Rp1-D21 (functional association only, not shown as direct physical interaction); RPM1D505V (functional association only)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced late (120 hpi) during Rp1-D-mediated ETI to Puccinia sorghi; induced early at 12 hpi in susceptible near-isogenic line lacking Rp1-D, reduced by 24 hpi and absent by 120 hpi
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In maize leaves under Rp1-D21 background and VIGS assays; transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defence, barrier formation, signalling, and regulation of apoptosis/programmed cell death
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Proteasome inhibitor MG132; pathogen-related ETI context; autoactive NLR-triggered HR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMIEL1 was identified as the causal gene at a maize HR modifier locus associated with Rp1-D21 severity. 2. Overexpression of ZmMIEL1 suppressed Rp1-D21- and RPM1D505V-induced HR, while silencing ZmMIEL1 enhanced Rp1-D21-mediated HR in maize. 3. ZmMIEL1 is a functional E3 ligase with autoubiquitination activity and its HR suppression depends on the proteasome. 4. ZmMIEL1 physically interacts with ZmMYB83 and lowers ZmMYB83 transcript/protein abundance. 5. ZmMIEL1 affects expression of several VLCFA biosynthetic genes, likely partly through ZmMYB83.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to tune defence-associated VLCFA-related responses and HR intensity in maize; could be considered for manipulating immune response strength, but direct nutrient improvement application is not established
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from separate study cited for ZmMIEL1 expression dynamics; no accession number reported in this paper
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary study focus is immune HR, not nutrient quality. VLCFA genes are downstream metabolic outputs linked to defence, and the paper does not measure VLCFA metabolite abundance directly.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB transcription factor with Myb-type HTH DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; Cytosol when interacting with ZmMIEL1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances Rp1-D21-mediated hypersensitive response in maize and regulates expression of a subset of VLCFA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of defence-associated VLCFA gene expression and HR, but not established as a master regulator
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of very long-chain fatty acid-associated gene network linked to HR
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defence, barrier formation, signalling, and regulation of apoptosis/programmed cell death
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FoMV-VIGS silencing in maize; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; coimmunoprecipitation; BiFC; immunoblot; RT-qPCR; ion leakage assay
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: KCS1 homolog; fatty acid thioesterase 2; fatty acid export protein 5; fatty acid export protein 5-like; additional VLCFA-related genes including KCS1; KCS2; KCS1-like; LTP2 were analyzed
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports expression of several VLCFA biosynthetic genes carrying AtMYB30-like binding motifs; silencing reduces expression of some expected targets, but some VLCFA-related genes show opposite/noncanonical responses
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of downstream VLCFA biosynthetic genes is supported for a subset; activity is negatively regulated by ZmMIEL1-associated degradation or transcript/protein suppression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physical interaction with ZmMIEL1 supported by co-IP and BiFC; no larger stable complex defined
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Rp1-D21-mediated defence/HR context; negative control by ZmMIEL1
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Autoactive NLR-triggered HR background; VIGS-mediated silencing context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Assessed in maize leaves under B73:Rp1-D21 background; transcript and protein levels increase when ZmMIEL1 is silenced
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; in maize leaves under HR-inducing Rp1-D21 background
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB83 is the closest maize homolog of Arabidopsis AtMYB30 and functions as a positive regulator of Rp1-D21-induced HR in maize. 2. Silencing ZmMYB83 suppresses HR and chlorosis in B73:Rp1-D21 plants. 3. ZmMYB83 physically interacts with ZmMIEL1 in nucleus and cytosol. 4. ZmMIEL1 reduces ZmMYB83 transcript and protein accumulation. 5. ZmMYB83 contributes to regulation of a subset of VLCFA biosynthetic genes associated with defence-related cell death.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modulating defence-associated VLCFA transcriptional programs and HR strength; direct nutritional biofortification value is not established
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transient overexpression of ZmMYB83 alone in N. benthamiana did not visibly alter Rp1-D21-induced HR, suggesting context dependence or requirement for maize-specific regulatory environment/interaction with ZmMIEL1.
